revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Itakiso shrine]][[ja:伊太祁曽神社]][[zh:伊太祁曾神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Itakiso Shrine
伊太祁󠄀曽神社 | image = Itakiso-jinja, haisho.jpg | caption = ''{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Haisho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}'' of Itakiso Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|12|6.1|N|135|15|0|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title, inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Itakiso Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Isotakeru | founded_by = | established = pre-{{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} | date_destroyed = | location = 558 Idakiso, Wakayama-shi, Wakayama-ken, Japan | website = {{Official|1=https://itakiso-jinja.net}} | architecture_style = | festival = October 15 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Itakiso Shrine'''|伊太祁曽神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Itakiso neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}} in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is one of the three shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on October 15.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Itakiso Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-kami|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotakeru-no-kami|qq=|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}'''| 五十猛神|}}, the son of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] and the god of forests and forestry * {{nihongo|'''Oyatsuhime no Mikoto'''| 大屋都比賣命 |}}, younger sister of Isotakeru, goddess of horticulture * {{nihongo|'''Tsumatsuhime no Mikoto'''| 都麻津比賣命 |}}, younger sister of Isotakeru, goddess of lumber and construction ==History== The origins of Itakiso Jinja are unknown. It first appears in the documentary record in an entry in the [[Shoku Nihongi]] dated 702 AD under {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|qid=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. The shrine was originally located on what is the site of [[Hinokuma Shrine]] but during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} was relocated to a place called "Anomori" near its current location, and then relocated again to its present site in 713. According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] and his son Isotakeru had been driven out of the land of [[Takamagahara]] and had landed in {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}. However, they did not like that land, and travelled by boat to {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo Province}}. There, Susanoo handed over the seeds of the tree he had brought from Takamagahara to Isotakeru and ordered him to sow the seeds all over Japan. Isotakeru, along with his younger sisters Oyatsuhime and Tsutomuhime, began to sow seeds all over Japan, turning the entire country into forests, finally arriving in the "country of trees", or Kii Province. In the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' record, the Itakiso Jinja is listed as a {{nihongo|'''[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]'''|名神大社||}} and is called the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shine is listed in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' dated 927 AD as a shrine of the highest Rank, and is stated to be the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Kii Province. From the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the shrine developed a close relationship with [[Negoro-ji]] and the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingi-SHingon|1=Shingon Buddhism}} sect of {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|qid=Q736311|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism in Japan}}. Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]],I it was listed as a {{nihongo|National Shrine, 2nd Rank|国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei Chūsha|Kokuhei Chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} in 1885. It was promoted to a {{nihongo|Imperial Shrine, 2nd Rank|官幣中社|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]}} in 1918.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a five-minute walk from {{ill|Idakiso Station|en|Idakiso Station|qid=Q11379273|lt=Idakiso Station|1=Idakiso Station}} on the [[:en:Wakayama Electric Railway|Wakayama Electric Railway]] {{ill|Kishigawa Line|en|Kishigawa Line|qid=Q6416586|lt=Kishigawa Line|1=Kishigawa Line}}. ==Gallery== 和歌山市伊太祈曽 伊太祁曽神社・一ノ鳥居 Shrine gate of Itakiso-jinja 2011.7.15 - panoramio.jpg|Torii approach to the shrine Itakiso-jinja Taikobashi.jpg|Taiko-bashi Itakiso-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden ==See also== *[[Ichinomiya]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category|Itakiso-jinja}} *[https://itakiso-jinja.net Official homepage] *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1050 Wakayama Jinja Honcho] *[https://en.visitwakayama.jp/venues/venue_501/ Wakayama Prefectural Government website] {{Isotakeru Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Itakiso shrine]] {{Commons category|Itakiso-jinja}} [[ja:伊太祁曽神社]] [[zh:伊太祁曾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11379271}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Kii Province]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wakayama (city)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
